title: jasminum meyeri-johannis
intro: Jasminum meyeri-johannis, also known as the Winter Jasmine, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Oleaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, antitussive, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which contribute to its health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure and prevent the formation of blood clots. In herbal preparations, the Winter Jasmine is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
